meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve can't debug code (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome can't debug code. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Analytics Setup to Solve Debugging Woes
content.seo_description: Stop guessing why your code breaks. Master advanced analytics setup with Antigravity and Claude Code to gain deep observability into your AI projects.
content.answer_block: You can't debug what you can't see. By instrumenting your application with event-driven analytics, you transform 'silent failures' into actionable telemetry. This checklist guides you through integrating high-fidelity observability, allowing you to trace execution paths, pinpoint bottlenecks, and resolve complex logic errors with precision rather than blind trial-and-error.
content.intro:
The screen flickers, the console remains eerily silent, and your application hangs in a state of suspended animation. You’re staring at a black box, unable to discern whether the failure is a network timeout, a malformed JSON payload, or a silent logic error in your AI agent's reasoning loop. This is the 'debugging wall' every developer hits, but the difference between a student and a senior engineer is the ability to illuminate the path forward.

True observability isn't just about logging errors; it's about mapping the narrative of your code. By leveraging AI-assisted instrumentation, you can turn your application into a self-reporting system. We are going to move beyond basic console.logs and build a professional-grade telemetry layer that gives you the god-mode view required to ship production-ready software.

steps.title: Define Telemetry Schemas
steps.description: Standardize your event structure before writing code to ensure cross-platform compatibility.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a TypeScript schema definition for analytics events including userId, eventType, payload, and traceId for distributed tracing.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Generate a telemetry/schema.ts file with Zod validation for tracking AI request latency and failure states.'

steps.title: Instrument the AI Agent Loop
steps.description: Inject observability hooks directly into your LLM reasoning chains to track token usage and logic flow.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Wrap my main agent execution function with a decorator that logs input, output, and execution time to an analytics provider.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Add analytics instrumentation hooks to the agent/orchestrator.ts file without breaking existing logic.'

steps.title: Implement Distributed Tracing
steps.description: Connect fragmented logs into a single coherent timeline using unique correlation IDs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Implement a middleware that attaches a correlation ID to every request and logs it to my telemetry sink.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Integrate correlation ID propagation across all API endpoints in server/index.ts.'

steps.title: Automate Error Reporting
steps.description: Capture the full stack trace and state context when an exception occurs.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a global error handler that catches exceptions, captures the current state, and pushes a critical event to the analytics dashboard.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Refactor error handling in the services layer to automatically emit events on failures.'

steps.title: Validate Telemetry Flow
steps.description: Verify that your analytics events are actually firing and reaching your dashboard.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a test script that simulates user interaction and logs the output of the analytics provider.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run --prompt 'Create a testing utility to verify telemetry events are correctly formatted and sent.'

pro_tips: Always log the 'before' and 'after' state of your data transformations; 90% of bugs live in the mapping layer.
Use sampling for high-frequency events to keep your analytics costs manageable during development.
Include the model version and system prompt hash in your analytics metadata to identify performance drift.
Set up real-time alerting for specific error codes rather than relying on dashboard monitoring.
Treat your logs as a first-class product feature; if a developer can't understand the log, the feature isn't finished.
content.next_steps: Once your telemetry is live, set up a dashboard to visualize your latency percentiles. Use these insights to optimize your most expensive API calls.
content.conversion_block: You now possess the architecture to see through the code and build with absolute certainty. Don't let your next project remain a mystery. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
